title: carphephorus subtropicanus
intro: Carphephorus subtropicanus, also known as the subtropical false goldeneye, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The subtropicanus plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Carphephorus subtropicanus can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
